title: Comedian Russell Kane Makes Theatre Debut as an Older Romeo
summary: Comedian Russell Kane is set to make his professional theatre debut playing Romeo in a new production of Romeo and Juliet. The play, a collaboration between the New Vic Theatre and theatre company Not Too Tame, will reimagine Shakespeare's classic with the star-crossed lovers meeting later in life, aged in their 40s. Kane, known for his stand-up comedy and multiple Edinburgh Comedy Award nominations, expressed his excitement to BBC Radio Stoke, saying he has been 'obsessed with theatre' and always wanted to perform on stage. He has previously toured three of his own plays, giving him experience with scripts and ensemble work. The 50-year-old comedian feels a deep connection with Romeo, describing himself as someone who needs to 'tame' his energy, and believes the older perspective adds a 'different flavour' to the story. The production, directed by Jimmy Fairhurst, runs from 17 September to 10 October at the New Vic Theatre in Newcastle-under-Lyme, coinciding with the venue's 40th anniversary. Kane sees this as a meaningful coincidence, as the theatre's age matches the protagonists' age in this adaptation.

background_read: Russell Kane is a well-known British comedian who has been performing stand-up for nearly 20 years. He has been nominated for the Edinburgh Comedy Awards multiple times. The New Vic Theatre in Newcastle-under-Lyme is a famous theatre that is celebrating its 40th anniversary. Not Too Tame is a theatre company known for innovative productions. Romeo and Juliet is one of Shakespeare's most famous tragedies, about two young lovers from feuding families. This new adaptation explores how their story might unfold if they met later in life.
Article_Structure: The article announces Russell Kane's theatre debut as Romeo in a new production of Romeo and Juliet. Main points include Kane's excitement, the unique twist of older protagonists, and the play's run during the theatre's 40th anniversary. The purpose is to inform readers about this upcoming event and generate interest. Evidence is primarily Kane's own statements, which are firsthand and credible. Author credibility: BBC News is a reputable source. Methodology: Information gathered through an interview with Kane and press release details.

perspectives.description: He is thrilled and feels the role is timely and meaningful.
The production marks the 40th anniversary and offers a fresh take on a classic.
